title: Antifragility

content.statement: Some systems get stronger from volatility instead of weaker.
content.question: Where in my business does volatility hurt me, and where could I redesign so it helps?
content.business_lens: Use barbell strategy (mostly safe + small high-upside bets); decentralize decisions; preserve slack to seize chaos.
content.life_lens: Design exposure so volatility benefits you more than it harms you.
sop: Map current exposures: what fragilities, what robustness, what antifragility?
Build optionality where you are fragile
Add convex bets (capped downside, uncapped upside)
content.explainer: Restaurants run brittle (one bad month kills them); R&D labs run antifragile (most experiments fail, but the rare hits more than compensate). Antifragility is built through structure: optionality, convex payoffs, slack, and barbell strategies.
questions: Where in my business does volatility hurt me, and where could I redesign so it helps?
Name one part of your business that would break under a bad month. Could you redesign it to instead get information or upside from that same stress?
What's your current equivalent of a barbell, mostly safe with a few small high-upside bets, versus one big bet with no room for a bad surprise?

definitions.def: A system that gets stronger under stress.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Convex Payoffs 6 (capped downside, uncapped upside on your bets), Optionality 7, Recovery Capacity 5 (how fast you bounce back from a shock). Score = (6 + 7 + 5) ÷ 3 = 6.0. Higher means stress and volatility tend to make you stronger over time; lower means shocks just accumulate damage with no offsetting upside.
content.closing_statement: Design for volatility to help you, not just survive it.

caption:
Principle Nº 325: Antifragility

Some systems get stronger from volatility instead of weaker.

Ask yourself: Where in my business does volatility hurt me, and where could I redesign so it helps?
